serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of raw pig kidney is approximately 85 g, equivalent to roughly one medium kidney lobe before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Pig kidney, raw — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All core macros, sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, and dietary fibre values sourced directly from HPB Foodo entry for Pig kidney, raw (cr_id F06090016). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, sugar, and micronutrient values not reported by HPB; supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, kidneys, raw (NDB No. 10710) where available; remaining fields set to -1 where no approved source reports the value.

gi_source.title: N/A — Pig kidney, raw contains negligible carbohydrates; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pig kidney, raw contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable for this food as it contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned in the University of Sydney GI Database for organ meats with zero carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. gi_category is set to not_applicable per Veritas rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy template numeric type requirements; it does not represent a measured GI of zero.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 121 mg/100 g with favourable potassium ratio (229 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Pig kidney, raw has a sodium content of 121 mg/100 g, well below the 200 mg/100 g threshold for hypertension concern, and its potassium content of 229 mg/100 g supports blood pressure regulation; however, patients should avoid high-sodium cooking sauces such as soy sauce or oyster sauce commonly used in kidney preparations, and the very high cholesterol content (319 mg/100 g) warrants monitoring in patients on antihypertensive medications that affect lipid metabolism.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate supports glycaemic stability; very high cholesterol (319 mg/100 g) elevates cardiovascular risk in T2D
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pig kidney, raw contains zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, making it glycaemically neutral and supportive of blood glucose control in Type 2 Diabetes; however, its extremely high cholesterol content of 319 mg/100 g is a significant concern given the elevated cardiovascular risk in T2D patients, and lipid profiles should be monitored regularly with consumption limited to moderate frequency.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high dietary cholesterol (319 mg/100 g) and high arachidonic acid (omega-6) content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pig kidney, raw contains 319 mg of cholesterol per 100 g — exceeding the daily limit of 200–300 mg recommended for cardiovascular disease patients in a single moderate serving — and is rich in arachidonic acid which promotes pro-inflammatory eicosanoid production; patients with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should limit consumption to occasional small portions and prioritise lean protein sources with lower cholesterol burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High arachidonic acid (omega-6) content amplifies inflammatory pathways; no anti-inflammatory factors present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pig kidney, raw is a concentrated source of arachidonic acid from pork organ tissue, which amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; it provides no positive anti-inflammatory factors (no omega-3, antioxidants, polyphenols, whole-food fibre above 3 g, or anti-inflammatory spices), and its very high cholesterol (319 mg/100 g) promotes systemic oxidative stress that worsens joint inflammation — consumption should be minimised in RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pig kidney, raw (also known as pork kidney) is an uncooked soft organ meat harvested from the domestic pig (Sus scrofa domesticus). It displays a characteristic dark red to reddish-brown colour with a smooth, firm texture and a distinctive bean-shaped form with an internal medullary cavity. Raw pig kidney has a strong, iron-rich aroma typical of organ meats. It is used across Chinese, Southeast Asian, and European culinary traditions — commonly sliced thin and stir-fried with ginger, spring onion, and sesame oil in Chinese cuisine (腰花炒), or braised and used in British steak-and-kidney pie. Nutritionally, pig kidney is a high-protein (16.5 g/100 g), low-fat (3.25 g/100 g) organ meat with zero carbohydrate, making it glycaemically neutral. It is exceptionally rich in vitamin B12 (18 mcg/100 g — approximately 750% of daily reference intake), selenium (190 mcg/100 g), riboflavin (1.91 mg/100 g), and iron (4.89 mg/100 g), making it one of the most micronutrient-dense foods available. However, its very high cholesterol content (319 mg/100 g) is a significant clinical concern for patients with cardiovascular disease, Type 2 Diabetes, or dyslipidaemia. Sodium is moderate at 121 mg/100 g. Pig kidney is not halal and not suitable for vegetarian or vegan diets. It is classified as a raw, minimally processed organ meat in the meat and alternatives food group.
common_user_queries: How much cholesterol is in pork kidney?
Is pork kidney good for diabetics?
Is pig kidney high in iron?
Can I eat pork kidney if I have high blood pressure?
What vitamins are in pig kidney?
Is pig kidney high in protein?

confidence_reason: Core macros, sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, and dietary fibre sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id F06090016, confidence 0.95). Fatty acid breakdown, B-vitamins, selenium, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water content supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 10710) for pork kidney, raw — an exact food match in an approved secondary source. All values are internally consistent and cross-validated between sources.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy — pig kidney is an animal organ meat containing no carbohydrate by composition (naturally zero). Sugar_g is naturally zero as pig kidney contains no sugars. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal-derived organ meat with no plant fibre. Trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy (confirmed zero). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: HPB does not report vitamin A for this entry and USDA SR Legacy reports retinol activity equivalents inconsistently for pork kidney across database versions; value excluded pending verified approved-source confirmation. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for pork kidney raw. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo; USDA SR Legacy does not provide a reliable value for pork kidney raw. Saturated_fat_g (1.09 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.68 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.55 g), trans_fat_g (0.0 g), sugar_g (0.0 g), vitamin_c_mg (9.4 mg), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.36 mg), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (1.91 mg), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (7.09 mg), vitamin_b6_mg (0.46 mg), vitamin_b12_mcg (18.0 mcg), folate_mcg_dfe (37.0 mcg), phosphorus_mg (257.0 mg), magnesium_mg (17.0 mg), zinc_mg (2.17 mg), selenium_mcg (190.0 mcg), water_g (78.0 g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 10710, Pork, fresh, variety meats and by-products, kidneys, raw. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable. The glycemic_index field is set to the numeric value 0 to satisfy template numeric type requirements per the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_source and gi_category document that this is a not-applicable sentinel value and not a measured GI of zero. Pig kidney is not halal (pork product). Ontology codes: FoodOn FOODON_03311349 and SNOMED CT 226934003 are cited as defensible matches based on published ontology documentation; FoodEx2 A0CKP is cited as the closest defensible FoodEx2 node for pig kidney raw — reviewers should verify against the current EFSA FoodEx2 browser if precision is required.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected glycemic_index from 0 to null to comply with q4 low/zero-carb rule for foods with negligible carbohydrates.

Iteration 3: Validator feedback confirmed glycemic_index must be numeric. Restored glycemic_index to numeric value 0 as required by the validator's numeric type enforcement. Updated gi_source and notes to clearly document that 0 is a sentinel value for a not-applicable GI, not a measured glycemic index of zero. gi_category remains 'not_applicable' to preserve clinical accuracy.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Pig Kidney, Raw (F06090016) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. The q4 failure from iteration 2 (glycemic_index must be numeric) is resolved in iteration 3 by setting glycemic_index to the numeric sentinel value 0 while documenting in gi_source, gi_category ('not_applicable'), and notes that this food contains negligible carbohydrates and GI is not applicable. Primary nutrient data sourced from HPB Foodo Singapore (exact match, confidence 0.95); secondary micronutrient data supplemented from USDA SR Legacy NDB 10710 (exact food match). All three mandatory health conditions addressed with clinical specificity. Multilingual naming covers Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. Alias consistency verified. Ontology anchors are defensible. LLM description is comprehensive and clinically relevant.
